verses.commentary: Sabbath grainfields. Disciples pick heads of grain — permitted by Torah (Deuteronomy 23:25) but counted as reaping under Pharisaic Sabbath rules. The legal grid encounters human hunger.
'Not lawful' — Pharisaic accusation cites their tradition's expansion of Exodus 20:10. The dispute is interpretive, not textual.
Jesus cites David at Nob (1 Samuel 21:1-6) — eating the showbread that was technically not lawful. Precedent: messianic figure under necessity exceeds ritual code.
Bread of the Presence, restricted to priests, given to David's hungry company. The Davidic king takes priestly bread. Foreshadows Jesus' authority over both throne and altar.
Priests 'profane' (bebelousin, βεβηλοῦσιν) the Sabbath by temple service yet remain guiltless. Temple work outranks Sabbath rest within Torah itself. The Torah is not a flat code.
'Something greater than the temple is here.' The Messiah's authority outranks even the temple. The startling claim is made obliquely.
Hosea 6:6 again (compare 9:13): mercy not sacrifice. They would not have condemned the guiltless if they had grasped the priority. Theological misreading produces ethical injustice.
'The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.' The institution serves him, not vice versa. Christological breakthrough: Sabbath becomes a function of his lordship.
Into 'their' synagogue — Matthew's increasing distance language. The synagogues belong to opponents now.
Man with withered hand. Pharisees plant the trap: 'Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?' They use a suffering man as a legal probe.
Sheep in a pit on the Sabbath — would they leave it? Even rigorous Pharisees made allowances for property. The argument exposes their inconsistency.
How much more a man than a sheep. Therefore lawful to do good on the Sabbath. The Sabbath is positively oriented toward mercy, not negatively oriented as inactivity.
'Stretch out your hand' — he obeys, the hand restored. Jesus does no work; the man stretches and is healed. Legal technicality outflanked.
Pharisees plot to destroy him. Sabbath dispute escalates to death conspiracy. The kingdom-restoration is read as kingdom-threat.
Jesus withdraws (anachoreo again) — strategic. Many follow, all healed. He keeps healing without confrontation.
Warning not to publicize him. The messianic silence preserves the trajectory toward the cross from premature acclamation.
Fulfillment formula. The unspectacular ministry style fulfills prophecy.
Isaiah 42:1-4 begins. 'Behold my servant... my beloved... I will put my Spirit on him; he will proclaim justice to the Gentiles.' Servant Christology defined. Gentile inclusion programmed in.
Not quarrel, not cry out — the unspectacular meek mode. The kingdom does not arrive by megaphone.
Bruised reed not broken, smoldering wick not snuffed. Tender treatment of fragility. Until justice is brought to victory — the soft method serves the strong end.
'In his name the nations will hope.' Gentile hope is the climax of the Isaiah citation. Matthew has been planting this from the magi onward.
Demon-possessed man, blind and mute. Three afflictions at once. Healed to see and speak — restoration of communicative humanity.
Crowds ask, 'Can this be the Son of David?' First time the crowd publicly asks the messianic question. The pressure builds.
Pharisees' counter-explanation: he casts out demons by Beelzebul. The miracle is admitted; the source is recast. Demonic attribution becomes their preferred narrative.
Reading their thoughts. 'Kingdom divided against itself cannot stand.' Classical political wisdom turned theological.
If Satan casts out Satan, his kingdom collapses. The accusation requires demonic civil war — implausible as strategy.
By whom do 'your sons' (Jewish exorcists) cast out demons? If the same activity is fine when they do it, the Pharisees indict themselves with Jesus.
But if by the Spirit of God — then the kingdom has come upon you (ephthasen, ἔφθασεν). Real exorcism is kingdom-arrival in person.
Strong man imagery. To plunder the strong man's house, first bind him. Jesus has bound Satan; the exorcisms are the looting. Cosmic battle visualized.
Neutrality impossible. Whoever is not with me is against me. Polarization is forced by the Messiah's presence.
Every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven — except blasphemy against the Spirit. The unique unforgivable. The boundary is severe.

Speaking against Son of Man — forgivable. Against the Holy Spirit — not forgiven now or in the age to come. The blasphemy is calling the Spirit's manifest work demonic. Persistent attribution of Christ's saving work to Satan is the unforgivable line.
Tree and fruit again. The Pharisees' speech is the diagnostic. Their reading of Jesus exposes their condition.
'Brood of vipers' — John's earlier phrase (3:7) recycled. Heart-overflow theory of speech: words leak the heart's contents. They cannot say good because they are not good.
Good and evil treasure inside the heart produces good and evil speech. Words are inventory checks.
Every careless (argon, ἀργόν, idle) word judged. Casual speech receives full judgment. The unweighed word is still weighed.
By your words justified or condemned. Speech is forensic. James 3 expands. The tongue is the courtroom evidence.
Scribes and Pharisees request a sign. Same accusers asking for performance to justify the prior performance. The demand exposes unbelief.
'Wicked and adulterous generation.' Adulterous because covenant-breaking, not sexual. No sign given except Jonah.

As Jonah was three days in the fish's belly, the Son of Man three days in the heart of the earth. Death and resurrection foretold using Jonah's near-death-and-emergence as type. Cross visible in advance.
Ninevites will rise at judgment and condemn this generation. They repented at Jonah's preaching. Something greater than Jonah is here — and is rejected.
Queen of the South (Sheba — 1 Kings 10) traveled far to hear Solomon's wisdom. Something greater than Solomon here. Gentile precedents shame Jewish unbelief.
Unclean spirit goes out — wanders waterless places. Exorcism without subsequent indwelling-of-Spirit leaves a vacuum.
Returns to 'house' — swept, garnished, empty. The cleaned-but-unfilled person is the danger.
Returns with seven worse. Final state worse than first. Moral application: 'this wicked generation' is the empty-house case. Reformation without regeneration recoils into worse bondage.
Mother and brothers wait outside. Jesus' family seeks audience while he is teaching.
Message relayed: your family is here. The biological claim made manifest.
'Who is my mother and who are my brothers?' Provocative question. The natural assumption of kinship priority is publicly broken.
Gesturing to the disciples: 'These.' Spiritual family redefined by relation to the kingdom, not by blood.
'Whoever does the will of my Father.' The doing — the obedient orientation — constitutes new kinship. The Messiah's family is enlargeable by faith and obedience.
